items.title: Despite wingback depth, Dest injury threatens USMNT's World Cup hopes
items.summary: Sergiño Dest insists his season isn't over after suffering a hamstring injury, but if it is, that's a headache the U.S. doesn't need for the World Cup.

items.title: Australia grants asylum to five Iranian players
items.summary: Advocates had been imploring Australian officials to meet with the team to secure their safety after the squad were seen making what appeared to be an SOS hand signal after their final Asian Cup match.

items.title: Sources: Pochettino on Madrid coaching shortlist
items.summary: Real Madrid have Mauricio Pochettino on the shortlist of candidates to replace Álvaro Arbeloa ahead next season, different sources told ESPN.

items.title: MLS duo banned for life for betting on own games
items.summary: MLS issued lifetime suspensions to Derrick Jones and Yaw Yeboah for violating the league's gambling policy after reviewing the results of an investigation.

items.title: FA Cup draw: Man City to face Liverpool in QF
items.summary: Manchester City will host Liverpool in the pick of the FA Cup quarterfinals from the draw on Monday.

items.title: Is there a right time for a Panenka - as Brentford pay the price?
items.summary: Brentford are beaten by West Ham on penalties as Dango Ouattara is left to rue a missed Panenka. Is there ever a right rime to take one?

items.title: Flick reageert op Xavi's interview: 'Ik ken de waarheid'
items.summary: Barcelona-trainer Hansi Flick gaf maandag een korte reactie op het explosieve interview dat Xavi in de Spaanse pers heeft gegeven. De Duitse opvolger van Xavi gaf aan te weten hoe de vork in de steel zit, maar was slim genoeg om op zijn tong te bijten.

items.title: Man City host Liverpool, Port Vale face Chelsea - full FA Cup draw
items.summary: League One Port Vale's reward for beating Sunderland in the FA Cup is a quarter-final tie away to Chelsea, while Manchester City will host Liverpool in a heavyweight showdown.

items.title: West Ham through to last eight but Brentford’s Andrews backs Ouattara after Panenka miss
items.summary: Given their inability to win a ­knockout tie in normal time, there can be little doubt of the physical impediment that West Ham’s prolonged FA Cup endeavours must make to their efforts of remaining in the Premier League. But, with an eminently winnable home ­quarter-final against Leeds United now upcoming, the chance of a rare trip to Wembley is the type of happy distraction any relegation-­threatened side can embrace. Momentum can provide a dangerous asset. Continue reading...

items.title: West Ham naar kwartfinale FA Cup na slijtageslag en waardeloze Panenka
items.summary: West Ham United is de achtste en laatste ploeg die zich geplaatst heeft voor de kwartfinale van de FA Cup. Maandagavond was de Oost-Londense club na een spektakelstuk inclusief penaltyreeks te sterk voor het West-Londense Brentford.

items.title: Draper wins to set up Djokovic clash at Indian Wells
items.summary: Jack Draper defeated Francisco Cerundolo to set up a fourth-round clash with Novak Djokovic in the BNP Paribas Open at Indian Wells.

items.title: Lukkien steunt Van Bergen: 'Hij blijft onomstreden voor mij'
items.summary: Bij zijn eerste kans was het zaterdag tegen Ajax direct raak voor Thom van Bergen. Dat is ook weleens anders geweest voor de spits van FC Groningen, die dit seizoen moeite heeft met scoren, maar desondanks onomstreden is voor Dick Lukkien.

items.title: Cruijff over roerige start bij Ajax: 'Soms is voetbal niet wat we hopen'
items.summary: Jordi Cruijff is nog maar net begonnen als technisch directeur van Ajax en heeft nu al de coach van het beloftenteam (Willem Weijs) én het eerste elftal (interim-trainer Fred Grim) vervangen. Daarmee toont hij aan harde beslissingen niet uit de weg te gaan.

items.title: Dit is het speelschema voor de achtste finale in de Champions League
items.summary: De Champions League gaat deze week verder met de achtste finale. Dit zijn de wedstrijden waar we voor dinsdag en woensdag naar uit kunnen kijken.

items.title: ‘They are safe here’: five Iranian women footballers granted humanitarian visas in Australia
items.summary: Home affairs minister Tony Burke confirms US president Donald Trump’s overnight comments on social media that the players had been ‘taken care of’ Middle East crisis – live updates Five members of the Iranian women’s football team have been granted humanitarian visas in Australia, with the prime minister, Anthony Albanese, offering assistance to the other players and saying “help is here”. The home affairs minister, Tony Burke, confirmed the humanitarian offer on Tuesday morning, hours after the US president, Donald Trump, posted about their plight on social media. Burke said the visas had been granted at about 1.30am on Tuesday morning, around the time of Trump’s social media posts, which first criticised, then praised, Australia. Continue reading...

items.title: Panenka disaster! West Ham punish Brentford in FA Cup shoot‑out
items.summary: Dango Ouattara was the penalty villain for Brentford as his failed Panenka in the shoot-out cost the Bees - and sent West Ham to the FA Cup quarter-finals at their expense.

items.title: Newcastle vs Barca preview: 'We can deal with best league in world' - Flick
Atletico vs Spurs preview: Richarlison to start with Romero and Spence back
Scholes 'did not intend to be offensive' to Carrick
items.summary: Paul Scholes says an Instagram story post about Manchester United interim head coach Michael Carrick was "in no way intended to be offensive" towards his former team-mate.

items.title: Relegation fight is priority for Spurs, not Champions League, says Igor Tudor
items.summary: Romero, Spence and Dragusin will return to the squad Pedro Porro says interim coach is ‘100% on the right path’ Tottenham’s interim head coach, Igor Tudor, called tonight’s ­Champions League last-16 first leg at ­Atlético Madrid a “beautiful game”, but admitted it was not the priority as they battle against relegation from the ­Premier League. Spurs finished fourth in the league phase, earning them direct qualification for the last 16 and a return to the stadium where they played in the final less than seven years ago. But a run of 11 matches without a win has left them one point above the Premier League relegation zone and Tudor under pressure just three weeks after taking over. He admitted: “Our first aim is the Premier League, this needs to be said publicly.” Continue reading...

items.title: Faltering Liverpool need a big night in Istanbul to revitalise their season
items.summary: Loss against Galatasaray in September summed up Slot’s struggles, creating a template he is still working to remedy It is not the first time Liverpool have come to Istanbul looking for unlikely salvation. There is not a final on the line as there was 21 years ago, but a last-16 tie against Galatasaray feels almost as decisive for their season and possibly for the long-term future of Arne Slot, even as he marks his 100th game as manager. It’s not even the first time Liverpool have come to Istanbul this season: their 1-0 reverse against Galatasaray in September was their second loss in a run of nine defeats in 12 games from which their season has never recovered. They are in much better form now, but will be without Alisson after he suffered a minor problem in training. Continue reading...

items.title: Howe calls Newcastle’s match with Barcelona ‘biggest game in club’s history’
items.summary: Newcastle host Barça in Champions League last-16 first leg Howe: ‘It’s an opportunity to grab a moment we never get again’ For Eddie Howe it was quite a statement. “Barcelona is the biggest game in this club’s history,” said Newcastle’s manager. “It’s massive.” Given Howe usually seems allergic to exaggeration it was a surprising way to approach Tuesday night’s Champions League last 16 first leg with Hansi Flick’s side. Continue reading...

items.title: Alisson injury not 'a big thing' despite missing Galatasaray
items.summary: Liverpool goalkeeper Alisson is ruled out of the first leg of their Champions League tie against Galatasaray through injury - but could return to face Tottenham at the weekend.

items.title: Should lower-ranked teams always play at home in the FA Cup?
items.summary: After some superb FA Cup fifth-round ties, BBC Sport looks at whether lower-ranked teams should always get to play at home.

items.title: Galatasaray vs Liverpool preview: Extent of Alisson's injury revealed
"Un jour, un exorciste est entré dans le vestiaire" : Silvio Proto a vu plus fort que l'encens de Bertaccini
items.summary: Adriano Bertaccini a fait beaucoup parler en chassant les mauvais esprits de Neerpede à coups de bâtons d'encens. Mais Silvio Proto a déjà vu plus fort.

items.title: 📷 Op tijd voor het WK: Rode Duivel volgt voorbeeld van Lukaku en De Bruyne en is terug na maandenlange revalidatie
items.summary: Het licht schijnt steeds feller aan het einde van de (revalidatie)tunnel bij de Rode Duivels. Ook voor Malick Fofana is de lijdensweg zo goed als voorbij.

items.title: Wat was er aan de hand in Dender? Jonathan Lardot legt uit waarom we zes minuten moesten wachten op VAR-beslissing
items.summary: FCV Dender EH en Sporting Charleroi deelden afgelopen weekend de punten. Al was er oponthoud bij één van de doelpunten van de thuisploeg. En toch ook eventjes paniek. Wat was er aan de hand?

items.title: Pour assurer le top 6, il faudra gagner...face à Anderlecht ou Bruges : "Ce serait historique pour le club"
items.summary: Malines s'est incliné 3-1 à La Gantoise ce week-end. Avec 42 points, le KV n'est toujours pas mathématiquement assuré de disputer les Champions' Play-offs. Il manque encore une victoire pour en être totalement certain.

items.title: Six minutes pour valider un but contre Charleroi : le problème technique qui a semé la panique dans le VAR
items.summary: Charleroi a été accroché à Dender samedi soir. L'un des deux buts inscrit par les locaux a donné du fil à retordre à l'arbitrage vidéo.
